<@U0A5PCF61GE>, please share samples here regarding the same issue
changed the statues to pending on partner but it still in my q
<@U0A5PCF61GE> As per the team mentioned, please proceed with closing the cases in order to receive new cases
<@U09H0N6EM43> yes I am trying but most of them are dispute related cases
should we close it as resolved ? that will not affect the claim status ?
<@U05CXDKQUT0>
<@U07KHJSLZ1A> Can you please check why pending merchant is still assigned to the agents?
<@U07KHJSLZ1A> will closing the SF case result in claim cancelation in CS Panel?
Hey <@U05CXDKQUT0> I don't think so no; but if we closed it, how do we reply back to the customer if the ticket does no longer exist (closed)?

Good Q <@U05CXDKQUT0> Thank you so much!

<@U03TDPTCC7Q>, I haven't tried closing a dispute ticket yet, but in regular emails that I close, and I get a response from the customer must be manually checked after the end of the shift to ensure that no response has been ignored. They are not reopened automatically upon receiving a reply.

+ I also noticed that as soon as the customer withdraws the dispute from Tamara App, the dispute ticket is automatically closed on my end.
<@U05CXDKQUT0>, yes, correct! After any cancellation SF case will be closed and the customer got email.
<@U03TDPTCC7Q>, is it possible to reply customer before cancelling the dispute?

Not all customers confirm that their issue has been resolved, although we see that it has been resolved. Therefore, we do not want to close the ticket so as not affect the status of dispute in CS-Panel.

<@U07KHJSLZ1A> I was asking about the other way around.... if the SF Case status is updated to "closed" by the agent -> will this trigger a claim cancellation in CS Panel?
But this question is invalid :slightly_smiling_face: because we did not build this integration, which is correct.

so Team, if you close the SF case, this will not impact the dispute status in CS Panel.
so don't worry, even if you close the SF case (even if by mistake), the CS Panel dispute won't be affected
Yes, but how can they update the customer through email of the actions taken, Aly?
which updates do you mean in specific?

So let's say agent closed a SF case that should be pending on partner, after the SLA the customer is eligible for refund, the actions was taken on a claim level. Now those updates, where would they update the customer if the initial case on SF is closed?

<@U05CXDKQUT0> I see. Yes, you’re right! There is only one way integration Tamara -> Salesforce. But that's a good idea to have. Despite the complexity that one case could be related to many disputes.

Once the dispute in CS Panel moves from Awaiting Merchant Response to Under Review (i.e. pending Tamara), the linked SF case will be updated to "Working" . <@U07KHJSLZ1A> correct me if I'm wrong. Do we send an automated email to the customer when the dispute moves from Awaiting merchant response to under review?
Yes, that's correct. Moving to `Under Review,` the only transition which doesn't trigger email sending
<@U03TDPTCC7Q> is it clearer now?

I'm aware of all the above Aly, my issue why should we close the SF case if it's "awaiting merchant response"?

> I have a lot of ''pending Partner'' cases. Should I keep closing them until I receive more emails, and will that affect customer claims?
> in SF pending cases stay with the owner. we are working on changing this workflow, for now, the team should close the case to get more work assigned
If these SF tickets are closed, when the claims moves from awaiting merchant reply to under review, the SF case is already closed; it won't move to working, no? Then how can communicate with these customers?

> <@U05CXDKQUT0> I see. Yes, you’re right! There is only one way integration Tamara -> Salesforce. But that's a good idea to have. Despite the complexity that one case could be related to many disputes.
<@U07KHJSLZ1A> I am confused here, this is already part of the requirements that we have shared and now is parked; are we saying we are going to enable that now? I believe now is the time to review the Notion page again.

Hey <@U0790PZCKQT>! In the initial technical documentation, we agreed only on Tamara → Salesforce syncing for dispute updates, which reflects the same approach we previously used with Zendesk.

As for Salesforce → Tamara syncing, that wasn’t agreed upon (at least as far as I’m aware). So first, we need to clearly define the expected flows and how this should work before moving forward.
<@U07KHJSLZ1A> let's schedule a call to have a walkthrough with the team on the dispute solution and SF integration. this will bring clarity and eliminate confusion
<@U05CXDKQUT0>, sure, let's do that :+1:

Hi team,
<@U03TDPTCC7Q> <@U05CXDKQUT0>
cases on "Pending on partner" are still showing on the agent side and preventing her from receiving new cases as per the limit reached to 100 cases. Please check
please close the case

done She is already informed
 thank you

Hey <@U07KHJSLZ1A> Can you please check why pending partner is assigned to agents? These tickets shouldn't be closed until the claim itself is resolved.

<@U05CXDKQUT0> If we close those tickets; how can we follow up with the customers when we get the claim updates?
<@U03TDPTCC7Q> if you have a case number, would be easier to explain

but generally speaking in SF, design is different than ZD (at least for now). if an agent updates a case to Pending status, it will remain with the agent.
For in-app disputes, if the dispute created passed AI check and got pushed directly to the merchant, the SF case will be created in the queue in Pending Partner status

Thanks <@U05CXDKQUT0> I'm aware that the flow is a bit different on SF where in-app claims goes directly to pending on partner if it passes the AI check, or if the customer has moved it manually to pending on partner. My question is why pending on partner is assigned to the team and it limits their email capped at 100 cases?
this is by design. pending cases in SF will stay with the agents
we will change this design to be similar to ZD (pending will go back to the queue) but this will require some time (~1 week)

We will have a gap until this is resolved. Specifically, there will be claims on CS Hub marked as “Closed” in SF. In such cases, when a claim moves from Pending Partner to Under Review (i.e., Pending Tamara), there will be no corresponding case in SF to transition from Pending Partner to Working, which prevents us from updating the customers accordingly.
CC: <@U0790PZCKQT>
<@U04L6LHLKAT> FYI
in this scenario, the SF case will be updated from Closed to Working <@U03TDPTCC7Q>

So “Closed” is not the final stage of the ticket lifecycle as it is in Zendesk? Previously, the ticket would move to Resolved and, after the defined timeframe, automatically transition to Closed, which marked the end of the lifecycle.

yes closed is not a terminal status like ZD.
SF doesn't have Solved, it only has Closed which is = solved in ZD

messages.text: Does the modification we make to the issue type of the dispute ticket (generated-system) that we receive in SF automatically reflect on the dispute status in CS Hub?

thread_replies.text:
If you have a question, please do NOT raise a Jira request; you can just type your question here. 
If you want to submit a "Bug" or an "Enhancement", you can click on the "Raise Request" :ticket: below. The team will look into your request and respond as soon as possible.
<@U03TDPTCC7Q> <@U0790PZCKQT>
No, <@U0AANS22HAS> You need to do this manually.
Do you mean that our change to the issue type field in the tickets in SF will not affect Claim reason field in CS-hub?
Please note that I'm talking about system generated tickets

Yes, Ahlam. There's no live integration between them as of now. If the customer creates an in-app claim with X issue type it will remain the same on (X) CS-hub. If you changed it on SF, it won't reflect on CS-hub, you need to do this manually when applicable.

The customer raise a claim with reason that doesn't match their issue, needs a new claim. (e.g., Item not as described), and it should be, item not delivered).
Only if the claim reason is totally off.

Thank you. I'm asking to verify, because the current issue that classifies disputes as PB caught my attention. I was concerned that any incorrect classification of ticket issue in SF would automatically trigger Tamara's to refund amount.

Yamen brought to my attention <https://tamarapay.lightning.force.com/lightning/r/Case/500Sg00000lmrSnIAI/view|support case (00150491)>, where a partner reached out to him and then he included the PC email to initiate the ticket, with the merchant also included in the thread.

first time working on SF and i am so confused
and how can i send someone for survey in SF?

Set the ticket status to “*Working*” and click *Save* after completing the ticket details. After the call is closed and the wrap-up is fully completed, transfer the case using the *Case Transfer* box on the right. You will then notice that the *Case Owner* changes from your name to the name of the team you transferred it to.

If the call is *inbound*, you can close it from *Omni Channel*. However, if it is a *call back*, click on the *VC* highlighted in green in the screenshot. You will then find the option “*Transfer to Survey*” on the right side. Clicking it will transfer the customer to the survey and the call will close automatically.
you can view in Case Events tab

Hello <@U05K6636RQR> <@U05CXDKQUT0> kindly need your assist here.

How can I send an Email to a merchant who doesn't have any cases on SF care,
If I call his number a new case gets created, but how can I create a new case without having to make a call.
This is so important so we can send Emails to merchants from our side.
<@U09GQGXJBL7> this use case is not implemented in the system. can you explain what is the use case for you to send an email to a merchant that has no case created?

• Merchant has some expired documents, we send him an Email requesting the updated docs.
• Merchant settlement was reversed due to Invalid/closed bank account but he never reach to us so we send him an Email requesting a new valid IBAN.
• Merchant settlement was On-hold due to some compliance issues, we send him an Email to inform him of the issue and request the needed docs.
These are all scenarios where the Merchant never contact us so we are the ones who reach out to him.  (no case available)
al these scenarios should be automated in the B2B salesforce

Are these actions not automated today in the other SF?
<@U0A2LJL0FPY>

There are automated comms if they do not pass the compliance checks based on the reason selected in the verifying KYB stage. As per my knowledge and <https://docs.google.com/document/d/1tWoXcl-3hup1M1jVOBnWMoWIKGXsv4vSp-EP5MVMeSA/edit?tab=t.0|this doc>, hold settlement cases also have automated comms.

Yes, merchant receive an automated Email once the case is created on his account/opportunity level, but what if I wanted to send him an Email _(for clarifying or other reasons)_ through the new SF CARE a week after or a month?
Even if the merchant received an automated Email I should be able to send him another Email if needed.
Or when his settlement was reversed and Finance team asked me to request new bank details from the merchant and he doesn't have any cases on SF Care.

Previously on Zendesk we can send an Email to merchant at any time, but now on SF Care we can't he must have a case open then we use the feed to send an Email.

Cases on account/opportunity level are deferent from cases on SF Care. he might have a hold case open but no cases on SF care.

<@U09GQGXJBL7> I am not sure what your process is with the PC team. The onboarding team uses gmail for such additional comms, and Genysis to call the merchant which usually has better engagement than emails.
<@U05CXDKQUT0> So will there be a way we can send an Email on SF Care without an existing case? or if we can have access to create new cases without making a call?

neither nor Sami.
there has to be an existing case.
<@U09C4QE0GQ4> <@U04U3SP2BFH> I'm not sure what process is being followed here by the team
sorry <@U0A2LJL0FPY> I tagged you by mistake

Hey <@U05CXDKQUT0> we don't create new cases unless its a social media escalation or something and the merchant hasn't contacted us & no valid phone number listed to call them, that's the actual use case scenario.

I’m also not fully clear on why we would need to follow up with a merchant after they receive the automated email for missing documents. <@U09GQGXJBL7>, could you please elaborate on the reasoning behind this? From my understanding, this type of proactive outreach is usually handled by the Onboarding team, who focus on re-engaging unresponsive merchants. Our role typically starts once the merchant reaches out to us, and in those cases there is already a Salesforce case available for us to respond through.

Hala <@U04U3SP2BFH>, for example, Finance team has a list of old reversed settlements that we need to update their bank details in order to reprocess, so my team leader gives me a task to contact these merchants proactively and update the needed details. Some of them I couldn't call so when I try to send them an Email I couldn't because they didn't have any open cases.
I simply asked here how can I send an Email if they don't have an open case. *("You Can't")* would've been enough for me.:handshake:

Thanks for clarifying <@U09GQGXJBL7>, i was interested in knowing the use case scenario, now it makes sense:pray::skin-tone-2:

<@U09C4QE0GQ4> lets discuss this together and see the best way forward

messages.text: *Escalating while handing 3 chats and 1 email*

thread_replies.text: Have a question? Ask it here — no Jira ticket needed.  Found a bug or have an enhancement? Click “Raise Request”  below submit it.  The team will review and respond as soon as possible.

This point may be impacting agent performance when handling escalations while managing 3 chats simultaneously.

Currently in SF, when an agent attempts to transfer a live chat to L2, *only the “Manager Escalation Team” appears, Other teams become available only after the chat session ends.* 

While this approach has its benefits, it requires additional time from the agent to complete the escalation *especially when a new chats gets instantly assigned to them.*

Previously, agents had the ability to handle escalations to all the L2 teams while handling a live chat, allowing them to transfer instantly once the session ended, without delay or chats gets overload.


*What would work best?* 
 enabling agents to have all the teams showing while the chat is active as its more effective than having 1 team showing 


Note: This is a very minor detail, but it has a significant impact on agent performance. 

Cc: <@U04S7UE7ULW> <@U04L6LHLKAT>

i escalated these 2 cases to the tech , and they are transferred back to me

this is a common issue in SF and i mentioned it many time here in different threads , to the seniors and my leader too

every time we escalate a case , the system or the tech (idk) transfer it back to the owner, so if the cx has an issue like refunds not processed , it will take more time to solve the issue because the cases get transferred back to the owner and the technical issue remains, we have high volume of calls daily because of this , this is a serious issue

PS: i cannot transfer the cases back to the tech team after that, unless it is a new case

from the error im guessing this is for GWS SMTP relay limits. Issue there is no workaround to increase this limit or unblock as this is natively reset after a day...... <@U05CXDKQUT0> if they can give email logs from salesforce to check the volume per day or best solutions is to have SMTP provider where we can set custom limit like sendgrid.

<@U05P3AASZ2M> <@U062BFC1XHV> implemented a change to have google workspace forward the emails to Salesforce instead of using the standard mailbox forwarding rule.
According to Shams, the standard forwarding rule has a limit. While the Google workspace doesn't.
Is this implemented? if yes, can I go ahead and switch off the mailbox forwarding rule?
Yes go ahead <@U05CXDKQUT0> only for customer care
fwding rule stopped on customer care
Q: why am I not seeing the emails in the Gmail inbox anymore? last email was 11 Mar.. <@U05P3AASZ2M>

As the forwarding from google we set it directly forwarding emails to SF  , to avoid creating double tickets in SF , but now since the forwarding from the email account display , it will keep copy in the inbox going forward
<https://tamarapay.slack.com/archives/C0AG88HEJAF/p1773265350172779>

Now that forwarding is OFF:
• :x: No duplicate tickets
• :x: No Gmail sending limit issue
• :white_check_mark: Salesforce still receives emails
• :white_check_mark: Gmail inbox keeps a copy

Now this the current set up

Customer email
      ↓
Google Admin Routing
      ↓
:one: Delivered to Salesforce (ticket created)
:two: Delivered to customer.care inbox (copy kept)

Agents needs to have access to edit on cases after escalating it. This case has no issue type or order details yet, it was somehow escalated successfully. Agent had to add comment to the case to clarify it to the team. Having the ability to add/edit on case after escalating it would be very helpful for both frontline agents & intended team. We won't allow case edit after escalation. It doesn't make sense from a workflow standpoint. Agents need to complete all actions before transferring the case

<@U05CXDKQUT0> Having the option to edit the case after it's escalated would be helpful to grantee a correct & valid escalation and it's easier when follow up. Considering the agent would be handling more than one case at time as the current capacity for chat & Email team is (3 chats &1 Email) and it's possible to miss 1 or 2 action.

messages.text:
Hello Team,
For outbound calls, there is no option to transfer the call to survey. Instead, the interaction details are displayed in place of the transfer option.
For call back requests, the call transfer option appears on the right side of the interaction page.
However, for outbound calls, only the interaction details are displayed. I tested a call on my phone number and found that when I ended the call, it was not transferred to the survey.
Kindly advise if there is an option to convert it, and please share the details with us.
Thank you.

thread_replies.text: Have a question? Ask it here — no Jira ticket needed.  Found a bug or have an enhancement? Click “Raise Request”  below submit it.  The team will review and respond as soon as possible.
Hi <@U08RZ05GQ76>, please advise on this^
<@U09CEKWRQH3> Can you please copy here the phone numbers samples called?

yes
966537147067
this my phone number , after i noticed that there is no option to transfer the call,  i did a test to check if its automatic transfer or not

<@U09CEKWRQH3>
That is because the call without selecting a queue, So the transfer can be done by the blind transfer button then selecting the survey

Another option, You can go to the Phone Wrap-up & Outbound -> Select Queue name from the search queues field 'CC - Phone - Outbound', Dialing on behalf of the queue will populate the survey buttons correctly

noted
thanks for your help :pray:

messages.text: *SF access for Care Content team*

thread_replies.text: Have a question? Ask it here — no Jira ticket needed.  Found a bug or have an enhancement? Click “Raise Request”  below submit it.  The team will review and respond as soon as possible.

We need access to Salesforce to view issue types and cases for both countries. At the moment, I can only see one App Launcher (Partner Care), and it opens to a blank page, so I’m unable to access any tickets or extract issue types.

messages.text:
Today, I handled three tickets reporting system rejection of linked ticket delivery, requiring time to resolve each:

Case Details:
1. Ticket 00338627 (Previous: 00338617): Original claim wasn't sent to the merchant. Action: Canceled first claim, submitted and linked a second claim, and linked the ticket to the customer's account (due to missing contact details).
2. Ticket 00338941 (Previous: 00338934): Claim was active. Action: Sent follow-up to both sides, and linked the ticket to the customer's account (due to missing contact details).
3. Ticket 00339640 (Previous: 00089352): Claim was closed. Action: Notified the customer, and linked the ticket to the customer's account (due to missing contact details).
System Issue:
In all cases, migrating/merging the notification tickets with their originals failed due to a system error (attached).

Attachments Show:
• An example of the notification email used to find the original ticket.
• An example of one case where the original ticket was not assigned to me.

messages.text:
The issue of overlap chats due to the multi agents in a single chat.

This issue appears in two main scenarios:
1. Changing the conversation Owner near its end: The responsible agent (Owner) of the conversation is changed right before the agent ends it.
2. Changing the conversation Owner while it's being worked on: The responsible agent (Owner) of the conversation is changed while the original agent is still actively working on it.
Case id in SF: 00355781

:update:
This error should have been fixed now, Can you please reply to this thread incase you face this similar error only

_Note: If still showing, Please do the 1st troubleshooting step first, make sure to do a hard reload (Clear Cache, closing and reopening salesforce tab)_

I'd like to suggest automating our workflow to make our case management more efficient.

Currently, when a conversation is closed due to consumer inactivity, the case status is still "Working." Is it possible to set up the system such that it automatically changes the status to "Unresolved - Unresponsive Customer" after the case is closed?

This would make sure that our reports are more accurate and spare the team from having to do updates by hand.
